definition: The first letter of the English alphabet, called a and written in the Latin script.
To give up or relinquish control of, to surrender or to give oneself over, or to yield to one's emotions.
Having given oneself up to vice; immoral; extremely wicked, or sinning without restraint; irreclaimably wicked.
The act of abandoning, or the state of being abandoned; total desertion; relinquishment.
To lessen (something) in force or intensity; to moderate.
The office or dominion of an abbot or abbess.
To shorten by omitting parts or details.
The result of shortening or reducing; abridgment.
To disclaim and expel from the family, as a father his child; to disown; to disinherit.
Of or pertaining to the abdomen; ventral.
The abnormal termination of a program.
The act of wandering; deviation from truth, moral rectitude; abnormal; divergence from the straight, correct, proper, normal, or from the natural state.
To incite; to assist or encourage by aid or countenance in crime.
To regard (someone or something) as horrifying or detestable; to feel great repugnance toward.
Inconsistent with, or far removed from, something; strongly opposed.
To endure without yielding; to withstand.
Continuing or persisting in the same state: lasting, enduring; steadfast.
Existing in or sunk to a low condition, position, or state; contemptible, despicable, miserable.
Burning fiercely; in a blaze; on fire.
Having the necessary powers or the needed resources to accomplish a task.
Not conforming to rule or system; deviating from the usual or normal type.
The state or quality of being abnormal; variation; irregularity.
On board; into or within a ship or boat; hence, into or within a railway car.
To end a law, system, institution, custom or practice.
The act of abolishing; an annulling; abrogation.
Worthy of, or causing, abhorrence, as a thing of evil omen; odious in the utmost degree; very hateful; detestable; loathsome; execrable.
First according to historical or scientific records; original; indigenous; primitive.
An early termination of a mission, action, or procedure in relation to missiles or spacecraft; the craft making such a mission.
The expulsion from the womb of a foetus or embryo before it is fully developed, with loss of the foetus.
Produced by abortion; born prematurely and therefore unnatural.
To be full to overflowing; to bristle.
In a circle around; all round; on every side of; on the outside of; around.
Physically over; on top of; worn on top of, said of clothing.
Producing abrasion; rough enough to wear away the outer surface.
A barangay of Dagami, Leyte, Philippines.
Side by side and facing forward.
a shelter; a cavity in a hillside; a shelter on the side of hill with an overhung rock as its roof
Beyond the bounds of a country; in foreign countries.
A state of being away or withdrawn from a place or from companionship
Being away from a place; withdrawn from a place; existing but not present; (sometimes) missing.
Free of restrictions, limitations, qualifications or conditions; unconditional.
In an absolute or unconditional manner; utterly, positively, wholly.
To include so that it no longer has separate existence; to overwhelm; to cause to disappear as if by swallowing up; to incorporate; to assimilate; to take in and use up.
The act or process of absorbing or of being absorbed as,
Of a language or dialect (a lect), being different from another lect in its intrinsic linguistic features (as opposed to extrinsic features such as social, political or legal status)
The act or practice of abstaining, refraining from indulging a desire or appetite.
An abridgement or summary of a longer publication.
The act of abstracting, separating, withdrawing, or taking away; withdrawal; the state of being taken away.
Contrary to reason or propriety; obviously and flatly opposed to manifest truth; inconsistent with the plain dictates of common sense; logically contradictory; nonsensical; ridiculous; silly.
That which is absurd; an absurd action; a logical contradiction.
Fully sufficient; found in copious supply; in great quantity; overflowing.
Improper treatment or usage; application to a wrong or bad purpose; an unjust, corrupt or wrongful practice or custom.
Prone to treat someone badly by coarse, insulting words or other maltreatment; vituperative; reproachful; scurrilous.
Hell; the bottomless pit; primeval chaos; a confined subterranean ocean.
The scientific and cultural community engaged in higher education and research, taken as a whole.
Belonging to the school or philosophy of Plato.
An institution for the study of higher learning; a college or a university; typically a private school.
To agree or assent [with to ‘a proposal, a request’]; to give way.
To cause to move faster; to quicken the motion of; to add to the speed of.
The act of accelerating, or the state of being accelerated; increase of motion or action; as opposed to retardation or deceleration.
One who, or that which, accelerates.
A higher-pitched or stronger (louder or longer) articulation of a particular syllable of a word or phrase in order to distinguish it from the others or to emphasize it.
To receive, especially with a consent, with favour, or with approval.
Worthy, decent, sure of being accepted or received with at least moderate pleasure.
The act of accepting; the receiving of something offered, with acquiescence, approbation, or satisfaction; especially, favourable reception; approval.
A person who accepts; a taker.
A way or means of approaching or entering; an entrance; a passage.
A coming to; the act of acceding and becoming joined.
Having a secondary, supplementary or subordinate function by accompanying as a subordinate; aiding in a secondary way; being additional; contributing or being contributory.
An unexpected event with negative consequences occurring without the intention of the one suffering the consequences, and (in the strict sense) not directly caused by humans.
Pertaining to accident and not essence; thus, inessential; incidental; secondary.
In an accidental manner; by chance, unexpectedly.
A shout of approbation, favor, or assent; eager expression of approval; loud applause.
To render fit, suitable, or correspondent; to adapt.
Affording, or disposed to afford, accommodation; obliging; helpful.
Lodging in a dwelling or similar living quarters afforded to travellers in hotels or on cruise ships, or students, etc.
A part, usually performed by instruments, that gives support or adds to the background in music, or adds for ornamentation; also, the harmony of a figured bass.
The performer in music who takes the accompanying part.
To go with or attend as a companion or associate; to keep company with; to go along with.
An associate in the commission of a crime; a participator in an offense, whether a principal or an accessory.
The act of accomplishing; completion; fulfilment.
Agreement or concurrence of opinion, will, or action.

A registry of pecuniary transactions; a written or printed statement of business dealings or debts and credits, and also of other things subjected to a reckoning or review.
The state of being accountable; liability to be called on to render an account or give an explanation; liability to be held responsible or answerable for something.
Obliged, when called upon, to answer (for one’s deeds); answerable.
One who renders account; one accountable.
present participle and gerund of account
To heap up in a mass; to pile up; to collect or bring together (either literally or figuratively), often gradually and without active intent.
The act of amassing or gathering, as into a pile.
The state of being accurate; being free from error; exactness; correctness
Telling the truth or giving a true result; exact; not defective or faulty.
In an accurate manner; exactly; precisely; without error or defect.
To find fault with, blame, censure.

To make familiar by use; to cause to accept; to habituate, familiarize, or inure.
Familiar with something through repeated experience; adapted to existing conditions. (of a person)
A playing card showing a single pip, typically the highest or lowest ranking card in a game.
A spicy and salty pickle in Indian cuisine.
To suffer pain; to be the source of, or be in, pain, especially continued dull pain; to be distressed.
To succeed in something, now especially in academic performance.
The act of achieving or performing; a successful performance; accomplishment.
Sour, sharp, or biting to the taste; tart; having the taste of vinegar.
To admit the knowledge of; to recognize as a fact or truth; to declare one's belief in.
The fruit of the oak, being an oval nut growing in a woody cup or cupule.
Pertaining to the sense of hearing, the organs of hearing, or the science of sounds.
To furnish or give experimental knowledge of; to make (one) know; to make familiar.
A state of being acquainted with a person; originally indicating friendship, intimacy, but now suggesting a slight knowledge less deep than that of friendship; acquaintanceship.

A silent or passive assent or submission, or a submission with apparent consent, distinguished from avowed consent on the one hand, and on the other, from opposition or open discontent; quiet satisfaction.
The act or process of acquiring.
To declare or find innocent or not guilty.
The act of fulfilling the duties (of a given role, obligation etc.).
An English unit of land area (symbol: a. or ac.) originally denoting a day's ploughing for a yoke of oxen, now standardized as 4,840 square yards or 4,046.86 square metres.
Sharp and harsh, or bitter and not to the taste.
An abbreviation formed by the initial letters of other words.
To, toward, or from the far side of (something that lies between two points of interest).
Initialism of the Australian Capital Territory: a federal territory of Australia.
Temporarily assuming the duties or authority of another person when they are unable to do their job.
The effort of performing or doing something.
To encourage development or induce increased activity; to stimulate.
Having the power or quality of acting; causing change; communicating action or motion; acting;—opposed to passive, that receives.
One who is politically active in the role of a citizen; especially, one who campaigns for change.
The state or quality of being active; activeness.
Someone who institutes a legal suit; a plaintiff or complainant.
A female who performs on the stage or in films.
relating to a person's acts or deeds; active, practical
In act or in fact; really; in truth; positively.
Quickness of perception or discernment; penetration of mind; the faculty of nice discrimination; acuity of mind.
Initialism of Anno Domini (borrowed from Latin); in the year of our Lord.
An old saying which has obtained credit by long use.
To make suitable; to make to correspond; to fit or suit.
The process of adapting something or becoming adapted to a situation; adjustment, modification.
One who is capable of adapting to differing situations.
To join or unite (e.g. one thing to another, or as several particulars) so as to increase the number, augment the quantity, or enlarge the magnitude, or so as to form into one aggregate.

A person who is addicted, especially to a harmful drug.

Causing or tending to cause addiction; habit-forming.
Pertaining to addition; that can be, or has been, added.
Well skilled; completely versed; thoroughly proficient
The quality of being sufficient, adequate or able to meet the needs.
Equal to or fulfilling some requirement.
To stick fast or cleave, as a glutinous substance does; to become joined or united.
A close physical union of two objects.
Said to wish a final farewell; goodbye.
Lying next to, close, or contiguous; neighboring; bordering on.
A word that modifies a noun or noun phrase or describes a noun’s referent.
Being in contact at some point or line; joining to.
An appendage; something attached to something else in a subordinate capacity.
The act of administering; government of public affairs; the service rendered, or duties assumed, in conducting affairs; the conducting of any office or employment; direction.
Of or relating to administering or administration.
One who administers affairs; one who directs, manages, executes, or dispenses, whether in civil, judicial, political, or ecclesiastical affairs; a manager.
Deserving of the highest esteem or admiration; estimable.
The commander of a naval squadron or fleet, regardless of formal rank.
A positive emotion including wonder and approbation; the regarding of another as being wonderful.
To be amazed at; to view with surprise; to marvel at.

As is acknowledged to be true; by general admission; confessedly.
To inform or notify of a fault; to rebuke in a serious tone; to tell off.
A rebuke by an authority that one has erred and should not persist in one's actions.

The transitional period of physical and psychological development between childhood and maturity, beginning at the onset of puberty and with an endpoint defined either legally (at the age of majority, such as 18 in many jurisdictions) or psychocognitively (at various ages, depending on individual experience).
Of, relating to, or at the age of adolescence; at the stage between being a child and an adult.
To take (a child, heir, friend, citizen, etc.) by choice into a relationship.
Befitting of being adored; cute or loveable.
To make more beautiful and attractive; to decorate.

The act of advancing; promotion to a higher place or dignity
Any condition, circumstance, opportunity or means, particularly favorable or chance to success, or to any desired end.
Arrival; onset; a time when something first comes or appears; the time when it is approaching.
A feeling of desire for new and exciting things.
Inclined to adventure; willing to take risks; prone to embark on hazardous enterprises; daring.
A word that modifies a verb, adjective, other adverbs, or various other types of words, phrases, or clauses.
Unfavorable; antagonistic in purpose or effect; hostile; actively opposing one's interests or wishes; contrary to one's welfare; acting against; working in an opposing direction.
The state of adverse conditions; state of misfortune or calamity.
To give (especially public) notice of (something); to announce publicly.
A commercial solicitation designed to sell some commodity, service or similar.

An opinion offered to guide behavior in an effort to be helpful.
To give advice to; to offer an opinion to, as worthy or expedient to be followed.
Someone whose job is to speak for someone's case in a court of law; a counsel.

A mythological shield associated with the Greek deities Zeus and Athena (and their Roman counterparts Jupiter and Minerva) shown as a short cloak made of goatskin worn on the shoulders, more as an emblem of power and protection than a military shield. The aegis of Athena or Minerva is usually shown with a border of snakes and with the head of Medusa in the center.
Living or taking place in the air.
Living or occurring only in the presence of oxygen e.g. aerobic bacteria.
The atmosphere of the Earth and the region of space around it.
Concerned with beauty, artistic effect, or appearance.
The study or philosophy of beauty.
A Cushitic language spoken in Ethiopia, Eritrea and Djibouti.
Receiving others kindly and conversing with them in a free and friendly manner; friendly, courteous, sociable.
Something which is done or is to be done; business of any kind, commercial, professional, or public.
An attempt to assume or exhibit what is not natural or real; false display; artificial show.
The act of affecting or acting upon.
Having affection or warm regard; loving; fond.
A written or printed notice to be posted, as on a wall; a poster; a placard.
A signed document wherein an affiant makes a sworn statement.
Someone or something, especially, a television station, that is associated with a larger, related organization, such as a television network; a member of a group of associated things.
Associated, related, or united; subject to a particular affiliation.
The relationship resulting from affiliating one thing with another.
A natural attraction or feeling of kinship to a person or thing.
To agree, verify or concur; to answer positively.
That which is affirmed; a declaration that something is true.
pertaining to truth; asserting that something is; affirming
A bound morpheme added to a word’s stem, such as a prefix or suffix.
To cause (someone) pain, suffering or distress.

To incur, stand, or bear without serious detriment, as an act which might under other circumstances be injurious; (usually after an expression of ability, as could, able, difficult) to be able or rich enough; to spare.
Away (from the home or starting point, physical or conceptual). (Often preceded by far, farther, or further.)
Impressed with fear or apprehension; in fear.
The continent that is south of Europe, east of the Atlantic Ocean, west of the Indian Ocean and north of Antarctica.
Alternative spelling of Africa: the continent south of Europe and between the Atlantic and Indian Oceans
The rear part (after end) of a ship or other vessel.
The care given to a patient during recovery from an operation or after hospitalization.
That which happens after, that which follows, usually of strongly negative connotation in most contexts, implying a preceding catastrophe.
The part of the day from noon or lunchtime until sunset, evening, or suppertime or 6pm.
Another time: indicating a repeat of an action.

The amount of time that some being has been alive, or that some thing has been in existence, as measured from its birth or origin until the present or until some other given reference point. (Often measured in number of years; alternatively in months, days, hours, etc.; see also the usage notes)
The capacity, condition, or state of acting or of exerting power.
A temporally organized plan for matters to be attended to.
One who exerts power, or has the power to act.
To make (an offence) worse or more severe; to increase in offensiveness or heinousness.

The act of collecting together, of aggregating.
The act of initiating hostilities or invasion.

The person or country that first attacks or makes an aggression; that begins hostility or a quarrel; an assailant.
Angry or resentful due to unjust treatment.
Having the faculty of quick motion in the limbs; apt or ready to move; loose-jointed.

A person who aids or assists.
Alternative form of ay: a cry of pain or distress.
To cause to suffer; to trouble, afflict. (Now chiefly in interrogative or indefinite constructions.)
Something which ails one; a disease; sickness.

The pointing of a weapon, as a gun, a dart, or an arrow, or object, in the line of direction with the object intended to be struck; the line of fire; the direction of anything, such as a spear, a blow, a discourse, a remark, towards a particular point or object, with a view to strike or affect it.
One who aims; one who is responsible for aiming.
A female given name from French.
The substance constituting Earth's atmosphere: a gaseous mixture of nitrogen, oxygen, and various trace gases.
In or carried by the air.
A vehicle capable of atmospheric flight due to interaction with the air, such as buoyancy or lift.
A company that flies airplanes to transport people and goods.
A passenger-carrying aircraft, especially one of a fleet operated by an airline.
The system of conveying mail using aircraft.
A powered heavier-than-air aircraft with fixed wings: a fixed-wing aircraft with at least one engine or electric motor.
An airfield (an area designated for the takeoff and landing of aircraft), including one or more runways and (for commercial airports) one or more passenger terminals.
A lighter-than-air aircraft that can be propelled forward through the air as well as steered.
Impermeable to air or other gases.
A wing of a building, notably in a church separated from the nave proper by piers.
An island in a river, especially the River Thames in England.
Of persons, of the same kin; related by blood.
A summons to arms, as on the approach of an enemy.

Used to express sorrow, regret, compassion, grief, resignation, or disappointment.
In Ancient Rome, a white tablet or register on which the praetor's edicts and other public notices were recorded.
In Spain or Latin America, a municipal magistrate who has both judicial and administrative functions.
Any of a class of organic compounds (such as ethanol) containing a hydroxyl functional group (-OH).

A chronic disease caused by compulsive and uncontrollable consumption of alcoholic beverages, leading to addiction and deterioration in health and social functioning.

A town in Usulután department, El Salvador.
Elementary algebra: A system for representing and manipulating unknown quantities (variables) in equations.
A male given name from Arabic.
Otherwise; at another time; in other circumstances; otherwise called; also known as; formerly known as.
The plea or mode of defense under which a person on trial for a crime proves or attempts to prove being in another place when the alleged act was committed.
A person, animal, plant, or other thing which is from outside the family, group, organization, or territory under consideration.
To make less heavy; to lighten; to alleviate, to relieve.
To form a line; to fall into line.
An arrangement of items in a line.
Having resemblance or similitude; similar; without difference.
Every individual or anything of the given class, with no exceptions (the noun or noun phrase denoting the class must be plural or uncountable).
A transliteration of the Russian or Ukrainian female given name А́лла (Álla)
To make quiet or put at rest; to pacify or appease; to quell; to calm.
An assertion, especially an accusation, not necessarily based on facts.
To state under oath, to plead.

The use of symbols which may be interpreted to reveal a hidden, broader message, usually a moral or political one, about real-world issues and occurrences; also, the interpretation of such symbols.
A popular instrumental dance form in Baroque music, and a standard element of a suite, generally the first or second movement.
An English and Scottish surname transferred from the given name derived from the given name Alan.

A disorder of the immune system causing adverse reactions to substances (allergens) not harmful to most and marked by the body's production of histamines and associated with atopy, anaphylaxis, and asthma; any condition of hypersensitivity to a substance.
To reduce or lessen the severity of a pain or difficulty.
A narrow street or passageway, especially one through the middle of a block giving access to the rear of lots of buildings.

Either of two species of large amphibious reptile, Alligator mississippiensis or Alligator sinensis, in the genus Alligator within order Crocodilia, which have sharp teeth and very strong jaws and are native to the Americas and China, respectively.
To set aside for a purpose.
The process or procedure for allocating things, especially money or other resources.
To distribute or apportion by (or as if by) lot.
To let one have as a suitable share of something.

A metal that is a combination of two or more elements, at least one of which is a metal, a base metal.
To refer to something indirectly or by suggestion; to invoke it by implication rather than mention.
The power to attract, entice; the quality causing attraction.
An indirect reference; a hint; a reference to something supposed to be known, but not explicitly mentioned.
To unite or form a connection between (people or things), as between families by marriage, or between states by confederacy, league, or treaty.
An alley in a formal garden or park, bordered by trees or bushes.
An Egyptian female singer or dancing girl employed for entertainment or as a professional mourner.
The seed within the drupe of a small deciduous tree in family Rosaceae, Prunus amygdalus, considered a culinary nut.
Very close to, but not quite.
At, to, or in the air or sky.

At or from a distance, but within view, or at a small distance; apart; away.
With a loud voice, or great noise; loudly; audibly.
The name of the first letter of the Greek alphabet (Α, α), followed by beta. In the Latin alphabet it is the predecessor to A.
The set of letters used when writing in a language.
Pertaining to, furnished with, or expressed by letters of the alphabet.
Of, relating to, or inhabiting mountains, especially above the timberline.
Prior to some specified time, either past, present, or future; by this time; previously.
In addition; besides; as well; further; too.
Of a voice or instrument, high pitch; especially, the octave above the top line of the treble stave.
Rose bengal dye worn by Indian women on the feet during festivals.
A table or similar flat-topped structure used for religious rites.
To change the form or structure of.
The act of altering or making different.
Happening by turns; one following the other in succession of time or place; first one and then the other (repeatedly).
In reciprocal succession; succeeding by turns; in alternate order.
Relating to a choice between two or more possibilities.
Though, even though, in spite of or despite the fact that: introducing a clause that expresses a concession.
The absolute height of a location, usually measured from sea level.
A musical part or section higher than tenor and lower than soprano, formerly the part that performed a countermelody above the tenor or main melody.
Regard for others, both natural and moral without regard for oneself; devotion to the interests of others; brotherly kindness.
A light, silvery metal extracted from bauxite, and a chemical element (symbol Al) with an atomic number of 13.

At all times; throughout all time; since the beginning.
Abbreviation of Amazonas: a state of Brazil.
To merge, to combine, to blend, to join.
A city, the county seat of Potter County, in the northwest plains of Texas, United States.
To collect into a mass or heap.
To fill (someone) with surprise and wonder; to astonish, to astound, to surprise.
Astonished or confounded with fear, surprise, or wonder; greatly surprised (often with ensuing adpositions e.g. at, with, or by).
The condition of being amazed; a state of overwhelming wonder, as from surprise or sudden fear, horror, or admiration; astonishment.

A diplomat of the highest rank sent to a foreign court to represent there their sovereign or country. (Sometimes called ambassador-in-residence)
Ambergris, the waxy product of the sperm whale.
The state of being ambiguous; the state of leaving room for more than one interpretation.
Eager or inordinate desire for some object that confers distinction, as preferment, honor, superiority, political power, or fame; desire to distinguish one's self from other people.
Having or showing ambition; wanting a lot of power, honor, respect, superiority, or other distinction.
Simultaneously experiencing or expressing opposing or contradictory feelings, beliefs, motivations, or meanings.
An unhurried leisurely walk or stroll.
An emergency vehicle designed for transporting seriously ill or injured people to a hospital.
The act of concealing oneself and lying in wait to attack or kill by surprise.
At the end of religious prayers: so be it.
Willing to respond to persuasion or suggestions.
An alteration or change for the better; correction of a fault or of faults; reformation of life by quitting vices.
A supercontinent consisting of North America, Central America and South America regarded as a whole; in full, the Americas.
A citizen or national of the United States of America.
A drink made of espresso coffee diluted with hot water.
In the middle of; in the center of; surrounded by.
A female given name from Latin, a less common spelling of Amy.
A gaseous, toxic compound of hydrogen and nitrogen, NH₃, with a pungent smell and taste.
Articles used in charging firearms and ordnance of all kinds; as powder, balls, shot, shells, percussion caps, rockets, etc.
Forgetfulness; cessation of remembrance of wrong; oblivion.
Of a person or thing: in the midst of and surrounded by (other people or things).
An asteroid that orbits between the orbits of Earth and Mars.
The total, aggregate or sum of material (not applicable to discrete numbers or units or items in standard English).
Of or relating to the class Amphibia.
A fully sufficient or abundant quantity of; enough or more than enough.
To render larger, more extended, or more intense.
In an ample manner; extensively; thoroughly.
To entertain or occupy (someone or something) in a pleasant manner; to stir (someone) with pleasing emotions.

In which the value of a data item (such as time) is represented by a continuous(ly) variable physical quantity that can be measured (such as the shadow of a sundial).
Having analogy, the status of an analogue; corresponding to something else; bearing some resemblance or similar proportion (often followed by "to".)
A relationship of resemblance or equivalence between two situations, people, or objects, especially when used as a basis for explanation or extrapolation.
Decomposition into components in order to study (a complex thing, concept, theory, etc.).
Of, or relating to any form of analysis, or to analytics.
Of or pertaining to analysis; resolving into elements or constituent parts
One who believes in or advocates the absence of hierarchy and authority in most forms (compare anarchism), especially one who works toward the realization of such.
The state of a society being without authorities or an authoritative governing body.
The science that deals with the form and structure of organic bodies; anatomical structure or organization.
One from whom a person is descended, whether on the father's or mother's side, at any distance of time; a progenitor; a forefather; a forebear.
Of, pertaining to, derived from, or possessed by, an ancestor or ancestors.
A tool used to moor a vessel to the bottom of a sea or river to resist movement.
A harbor, river, or offshore area that can accommodate a ship at anchor, either for quarantine, queuing, or discharge.
Having lasted from a remote period; having been of long duration; of great age, very old.
Either of the symbols ⟨⸔⟩, ⟨⸕⟩ found in marginal notes to mark missing text.
As a coordinating conjunction; expressing two elements to be taken together or in addition to each other.
A surname from Spanish [in turn from Basque].
A short account of a real incident or person, often humorous or interesting.

A medical condition in which the capacity of the blood to transport oxygen to the tissues is reduced, either because of too few red blood cells, or because of too little hemoglobin, resulting in pallor and fatigue.

An artificial method of preventing sensation, used to eliminate pain without causing loss of vital functions, by the administration of one or more agents which block pain impulses before transmitted to the brain.
Again, once more; afresh, in a new way, newly.
An incorporeal and holy or semidivine messenger from a deity or other divine entity, traditionally depicted as a youthful, winged figure in flowing robes.
Belonging to, or proceeding from, angels; resembling, characteristic of, or partaking of the nature of, an angel.

A strong and unpleasant feeling of displeasure, hostility, or antagonism, usually combined with an urge to yell, curse, damage or destroy things, or harm living beings, often stemming from perceived provocation, hurt, threat, insults, unfair or unjust treatment, or an undesired situation.
